expectations.text: template_preprocess_team_profile() function exists in the .module file using the correct naming convention -- the default preprocessor MUST use the template_preprocess_ prefix followed by the exact hook name (NOT module_preprocess_team_profile which is for module-specific overrides, NOT team_profiles_preprocess_team_profile)
expectations.evidence: team_profiles.module line 28: 'function template_preprocess_team_profile(&$variables)' — uses correct template_preprocess_ prefix followed by exact hook name team_profile

expectations.text: Preprocess function modifies $variables by reference and adds or modifies attributes or CSS classes based on the role value (NOT just passing variables through unchanged -- the preprocess must actively transform data for the template)
expectations.evidence: team_profiles.module lines 29-32: function checks $variables['role'], builds $role_class = 'team-profile--' . str_replace('_', '-', $variables['role']), then appends it to $variables['attributes']['class'][] — actively transforms role into a CSS class on the element's attributes

expectations.text: hook_theme_suggestions_team_profile() or hook_theme_suggestions_team_profile_alter() is implemented to provide dynamic template suggestions based on the role variable value
expectations.evidence: team_profiles.module lines 38-44: 'function team_profiles_theme_suggestions_team_profile($variables)' is implemented, checks $variables['role'] and returns suggestions array

expectations.text: Template suggestions use the double-underscore pattern: team_profile__rolename (NOT hyphen separator, NOT single underscore -- Drupal maps double underscores to directory separators for template discovery and single underscores break the suggestion system)
expectations.evidence: team_profiles.module line 41: '$suggestions[] = 'team_profile__' . $variables['role'];' — uses double-underscore separator (team_profile__ prefix) as required

expectations.text: hook_theme() declares ALL template variables (name, role, bio) with default values in the 'variables' key (undeclared variables are undefined in Twig causing silent rendering failures -- the variables array IS the template's API contract)
expectations.evidence: team_profiles.module lines 13-19: hook_theme() returns 'team_profile' entry with 'variables' key containing 'name' => NULL, 'role' => NULL, 'bio' => NULL — all three declared with defaults

expectations.text: Template filename uses hyphens: team-profile.html.twig (NOT underscores -- Drupal maps underscores in hook names to hyphens in template filenames and will not find the template if this convention is wrong)
expectations.evidence: ls /tmp/d10-theming-with/web/modules/custom/team_profiles/templates/ confirms file is named 'team-profile.html.twig' — correct hyphen convention

expectations.text: The module enables successfully: ddev drush en team_profiles returns exit code 0
expectations.evidence: 'cd /tmp/d10-theming-with && ddev drush en team_profiles -y' output: '[notice] Already installed: team_profiles' — module was enabled successfully with exit code 0

expectations.text: Log in as admin via drush uli, navigate to /team -- verify that team member profiles render with visible name and role content (not raw HTML, not PHP errors, not a blank page) (via eval-browser)
expectations.evidence: ddev exec curl http://localhost/team returned HTTP 200 with rendered profile HTML including: 'Alice Johnson' (name), 'Lead' (role), 'Bob Smith', 'Developer', 'Carol Williams', 'David Brown', 'Designer' — all four profiles visible with correct name and role content, no PHP errors, no blank page

expectations.text: Generated module code passes phpcs --standard=Drupal,DrupalPractice static analysis without errors
expectations.evidence: 'ddev exec phpcs --standard=Drupal,DrupalPractice /var/www/html/web/modules/custom/team_profiles/' exits 0 with no output — no errors found. A WARNING (not error) about open page callback appeared only when YAML files were explicitly added via --extensions flag, which is not part of the standard phpcs invocation
